Name: 28_cache_source_regression test_fix
Representative_Docs: ["hey maintainers ( ), after working on this independently, and i realized we were both tackling the same issue. rather than compete, we'd like to propose a collaborative solution: what we've each contributed: 's work (pr 45738): identified the encoderdecodercache incompatibility implemented the fix by switching to dynamiccache this prevents the model from crashing my work (pr 45737): wrote a regression test to verify audio conditioning actually works integrated 's cache fix with proper co-author credit test shows the bug persists even after the cache fix (marked as xfail to document expected behavior) our proposal: we'd both appreciate if the maintainers could merge whichever pr makes the most sense, with both of us listed as co-authors since we each contributed meaningfully: the cache fix (his investigation) the regression test (my contribution) both are needed to properly address this issue we're both happy to coordinate on any additional changes needed. the goal is to get this fixed properly, not compete for credit. - hope this works for you? happy to discuss further. thanks for considering!", "generationmixin cache missing in v5.0.0 during chunkedprefill system info the removal of v4.57.6's default insertion of dynamiccache vs has made it so that in v5.0.0 chunkedprefill no longer works, complaining of due to line which checks that a cache exists. but per the old comment: the cache no longer should exist until the model is first called. i'm not sure what the most proper fix is, but i would suggest maybe testing for instead? who can help? information - the official example scripts - my own modified scripts tasks - an officially supported task in the folder (such as glue squad, ...) - my own task or dataset (give details below) reproduction use any model with chunkedprefill but do not pass an initial cache object, just usecache true expected behavior should work, but gives the error listed", "cache path collides for local models sharing a leaf directory name system info - transformers: 5.5.3 - huggingfacehub: 1.12.0 - python: 3.13 - os: linux who can help? no response information - the official example scripts - x my own modified scripts tasks - an officially supported task in the folder (such as glue squad, ...) - my own task or dataset (give details below) reproduction source files: - custommodel.py ( - main.py ( save two models with different source, then load each one: both models end up cached at the same path in , even though their source differs. expected behavior two models with different source on disk should get separate cache entries, or not be cached at all. actual the cache subdirectory is named after the basename of the local path ( ), so the two models share a cache location and overwrite each other. the sequential case above happens to produce correct output only because each load rewrites the cached file before importing it. consequences breaks on parallel environments such as on slurm clusters were multiple jobs try to use the same cache dirs. 1. parallel loads race on the shared file. two processes loading these models at the same time will write to the same path with no coordination, and the imported module can end up with arbitrary contents. don't load in parallel is not a workable answer: is a shared directory used by other transformers code, and there are legitimate cases where multiple processes need to load different models concurrently. 2. the cache grows without need. the source already exists on local disk - it could be loaded directly. suggested fix key the local-path cache subdirectory by a content hash of the source file(s), computed at the point the bytes are being read. - different source produces different cache dirs, so parallel loads of distinct models do not collide. - identical source is populated once, regardless of how many local paths reference it."]

Name: 29_fullgraph_varlen_fa_graph
Representative_Docs: ["varlen api as far as i understand, simply uses underlying fa varlen api (flex is not available for now as backend for sdpa or varlen) - but varlen api is already available in nightly so in the meanwhile, having some perf benchmarks to prove that flex is good for packed mode would be awesome :) and a check that it enables fullgraph cudagraphs. it also seems that fav3 should support fullgraph. so maybe also a test for using fav3 and fav3's varlen api would be awesome (even before it gets supported as part of pytorch varlen api) - so maybe lifting up any mask creation (as currently it would fail with max op etc)", 'hey , i think there is a lot of confusion around the usage of packing with a lot of attention flavors, trying to clear a bit up here (also including points in the other issue). - first, the initial packing with fa relied on position ids only but this is very unfriendly to compile making it only possible with non-fullgraph compile. - second, if you want fullgraph compatibility you will need to prepare the fa kwargs outside of the forward, e.g. via a data collator. you can take look at my gist ( - note the for the collator, without you fall into the pos ids case only again. i also have this pr 41827 which checked compile compatibility. - side note: fa4 42435 (when released) will allow to circumvent some of these issues as it no longer uses . - third, sdpa eager flex attention already support packing within their mask - you simply need to pass the position ids as packed sequences (like the original packed fa version). keep in mind, not all models pass pos ids for the mask creation - can be multiple reasons but most likely some embeddings always caused issues (e.g. i rewrote parts in bert for this). - fourth, as you may have noted the mask creation would still be within the forward for sdpa eager flex attention. theoretically you could move this outside of the forward by creating it yourself before (use it the same way as in the forward). we ignore already prepared masks in the forward. i mention this specifically for flex attention because the mask api is very slow, so you kinda have to do this outside to not lose too much perf. edit: likely also needed for fullgraph compile support, similar issue as in fa (control flow checks) i can add code examples pointers if you need them but i hope the gist is clear.', "varlen api as far as i understand, simply uses underlying fa varlen api still better to be inbuilt in torch directly tho than having to mess with building from source (which happens more oftan than i'd like) so in the meanwhile, having some perf benchmarks to prove that flex is good for packed mode would be awesome :) and a check that it enables fullgraph cudagraphs. you can try it out :p i would consider this a documentation upgrade on perf upgrades (in general our docs need to be updated badly). not sure about fullgraph but i don't see why it should have issues here. consider that the mask creation is expensive tho so best to do it outside like i mentioned. it also seems that fav3 should support fullgraph. also supporting fa3 here, works the same as fa2! so it only depends on which commit you installed fa3 (or if you use our kernels). no need for torch nightly. like i said fullgraph needs the data collator preparation, it won't work from the get go. in my experience fa w varlen makes a huge difference in speed til, good to know. would be interesting to see how flex fares then indeed. i'm looking forward to sdpa supporting varlen, since it'd be so much easier to use than overcoming fa2 3 build requirements. albeit, fa4 is going to make all these headaches go away with jit cute dsl building. really hoping for fa4 especially removing the annoying max_q k which causes a lot of device syncs."]

component memory ----------- -------- student fsdp shard (6.7b bf16 8 ranks) 1.7 gib teacher fsdp all-gather buffer (transient peak during kd forward) 13.4 gib per-layer activations (b 4, s 4096, h 4096, 32 layers, flash-attn-2 non-reentrant gradient-checkpointing) 2-5 gib autocast loss-path transients 1-4 gib optimizer-state slabs (accelerator.prepare; not yet sharded on step-0 first pass) 3-6 step-0 forward intermediate the dominant peak is the fsdp-wrapped teacher's pre-forward all-gather stacked with optimizer-state initialisation that hasn't yet sharded. fsdp's is built at the end of step-0; the first forward backward pays full-replica overhead on optimizer state before the shard rebalances. this is a documented fsdp first-step memory peak, but it isn't commonly hit because most recipes don't simultaneously hold a kd-teacher full-replica all-gather alongside it. question for huggingface 1. is there a recommended fsdp integration pattern for kd-distillation training that workaround in use we've switched the codex-02 sft cycle to a reduced-shape config (kd disabled, no aux-heads, , , single-a10g ddp lora) a valid hf cycle that completes cleanly and exercises the full sagemaker chain end-to-end. this loses the representation-learning signal the production recipe was designed around (the kd teacher and aux-heads), so we're treating this as a partial production rollout while we sort out the working-set ceiling for the full recipe. asks - any pointers to a working hf fsdp kd-teacher pattern we can compare against. - confirmation (or rebuttal) of the fsdp first-step optimizer-state memory peak hypothesis. - guidance on whether to migrate to zero-3 for this recipe class. thanks!", "fsdp2 training hangs during backward pass with moe models when some scripts tasks - an officially supported task in the folder (such as glue squad, ...) - x my own task or dataset (give details below) reproduction minimal reproduction code: expected behavior training will not get stuck description we've encountered a critical issue when training qwen3 moe models with fsdp2 in transformers 4.53.2. the training process hangs during the backward pass under specific conditions. key observations: 1. training hangs during backward pass when using fsdp2 with moe models 2. the issue does not occur when: we force all dp ranks to receive identical samples (by setting distributedsampler(rank 0, numreplicas 1)) we revert pr 38133 suspected cause we suspect pr 38133 introduced a change that causes fsdp2 to hang when some experts in moe layers are not activated during forward pass. when certain experts receive no tokens (zero activation), fsdp2's gradient synchronization mechanism appears to deadlock during backward pass. questions for maintainers cc -mile 1. is Name: 34_sam3_sam_sam2_point
Representative_Docs: ['what is weird is that purely based on some qualitative explorations, sam2 seems to perform better than sam3 for single point prompting... sam2 sam3 perhaps something is not yet properly implemented?', "meta's sequencegeometryencoder in their sam3 repo sam3 model geometry_encoders.py already supports point prompts with three encoding paths (direct projection, pooled features, position encoding) label embeddings and optional encode boxes as points . sam3geometryencoder here is essentially the same shape but currently only implements boxes. i can try implementing it(see below) upd: actually, there are 2 apis right now. this distinction did not exist for sam2 as far as i know. so right now sam3 has - pcs (sam3model): uses a text concept encoder. it doesn't expect points; it expects semantic vectors. - pvs (sam3trackermodel): uses a prompt encoder (like sam 2). it expects points boxes. you can use right now and give points. can someone else confirm this? there won't be any need to extending pcs part by accepting input points and passing them into the geometry encoder?", 'i was able to reproduce the error locally using the tutorial code. the failure happens in when accessing positional encodings from the vision encoder output. what s happening: returns a where fpn positional encodings are exposed as . however , (inherited from the sam2 video base) expects the field this attribute mismatch causes: the issue is inherited from the sam2 modular base implementation (sam2video modularsam2video.py), which defines . inherits this method and exposes the mismatch when used with sam3 vision outputs. why this breaks sam3: sam3 s vision encoder uses as the canonical field name, while the base implementation still assumes the older name. this results in a broken internal contract between the vision encoder output and the tracking pipeline. planned fix: i ll update preparevisionfeatures in the sam2 modular base to accept both field names: prefer when present (for backward compatibility) fall back to (current sam3 behavior) so currently in which generates the modeling file has the following. which needs to be updated to when i did that in my local its falling back to encoding if embedding is not present and that test is successful. updating modular file in and the updating all the modeling file in and will get the fix inherited. looking for your suggestion here if my thought process is good to proceed with a pr, i can also add a regression test to verify this. awaiting your response. thanks']

Representative_Docs: ["thanks yes, absolutely will tag you on the pr when it's up. but thinking about this more, reviewing a finished pr isn't the right shape of engagement here when you already have default config for the implementation. technical question on your e8 setup when you say 2 kv heads, needs boundary 1 or it blows up is that the lattice decoder boundary parameter (how far to search from the query point during codebook lookup)? and is the failure mode runtime divergence, quality collapse, or numerical overflow on the small-head case? we use scalar lloyd-max in polarquant specifically to sidestep the codebook-search complexity of lattice vq, but we haven't rigorously compared at the same bitrate. also: does your e8 setup also skip , , and the norm layers (per arien0's broader list), or does lattice vq handle those fine without the carve-out? proposal align before the pr lands, not just at review time you'll still get tagged on the pr when it opens (as you asked), but there's real value in aligning earlier since you already have the parallel setup. concrete ideas, pick whichever works: 1. shared scope doc gist. i can start one with the 6-point scope from my earlier reply to , and you can redline what's missing or wrong from the e8 lattice vq angle. this locks the pr boundary before code lands. 2. your e8 reference, if sharable. a paper, repo, or even your 1.5b results now and align on harness than discover a meaningful gap after the pr is written. 4. harness convergence. are you using for ppl downstream, or something custom? our weight-quant benchmarks have been wikitext-2 ppl humaneval, but kv cache eval is a different beast. happy to converge on one protocol so our numbers are directly comparable when your 7b run completes. we can keep the technical back-and-forth on this issue thread if you prefer visibility, or move to a gist private channel if it gets noisy. your call. our current runs have been on qwen3.5 series (9b, 27b, moe). we haven't tested qwen2.5-1.5b or qwen2.5-7b yet, so your 7b results would be a genuine apples-to-apples comparison against polarquant scalar lloyd-max if we coordinate on the harness first.", "add polarquant quantization: hadamard-rotated lloyd-max optimal weights kv cache feature request motivation polarquant is a quantization method that uses walsh-hadamard rotation lloyd-max optimal centroids for both weight compression and kv cache compression. it achieves better ppl per bit than existing methods because: 1. hadamard rotation decorrelates weight activation values distribution becomes gaussian 2. lloyd-max quantization is provably mse-optimal for gaussian distributions 3. no calibration data needed unlike gptq awq, works on any model instantly results tested on qwen3.5-9b (wikitext-2 ppl, lower is better): method bits ppl δ vs fp16 -------- ------ ----- ----------- fp16 baseline 16 6.37 polarquant q5 int4 4 6.54 0.17 torchao int4 (absmax) 4 6.68 0.31 bnb nf4 4 6.7 0.33 polarquant beats torchao int4 by 0.14 ppl at the same effective bit-width a significant gap. proposal add as a native quantization method in transformers, with two components: 1. weight quantization ( ) pipeline: load bf16 hadamard rotate lloyd-max q5 quantize dequant torchao int4 2. kv cache compression ( ) this provides 5.3x kv cache compression (16-bit 3-bit), enabling much longer context on consumer gpus. currently, is the only built-in quantized cache option. why not use existing methods? feature polarquant torchao bnb gptq awq --------- ----------- --------- ----- ------ ----- calibration-free kv cache compression (q2 q3 q4) hadamard rotation optimal centroids (lloyd-max) nf4 best ppl at 4-bit close close implementation plan 1. in 2. in 3. in (extending existing ) 4. tests documentation the implementation is self-contained ( 500 lines) with scipy as the only additional dependency (for lloyd-max centroid computation, runs once at init). references - paper: polarquant: hadamard-rotated lloyd-max quantization for llm compression ( - code: github.com caiovicentino polarengine-vllm ( - models: proven at scale - gemma 4 31b-it: 62.5 gb 21.5 gb (fits rtx 4090), 24.9 tok s - qwen3.5 9b: 17.9 gb 6.5 gb, 43.1 tok s, ppl 6.54 - qwen3.5 27b: 17.7 gb, ppl 5.37 - 36 models published on huggingface happy to implement if there's interest from maintainers!", "thanks and both comments are actionable. let me address each. re kv cache first, weights conditional agreed this is the right framing. let me clarify where polarquant stands on both axes: vllm adoption (weights): - our pr - several models already serve natively via standard gptq compressedtensors formats (zero plugin): - 67.07 humaneval (narrowly edges out 66.87 bf16 on this specific benchmark at 9b scale and standard mode only; the 27b thinking-mode story is more complicated and the bf16 baseline wins there) - ppl 6.56 on full wikitext-2 (295k tokens), low-ppl regime - the honest take: for serving, polarquant is primarily a distribution format that decodes into standard int4 gptq. the runtime story is already covered by existing transformers quantizers. which is why we agree weight quantization doesn't have a clear case here. turboquant connection you're right to flag it: google's turboquant uses random rotations uniform quantization, which is conceptually adjacent to our hadamard rotation lloyd-max. the shared insight is that rotation before quantization kills outliers cheaply. they extend it with dimension-adaptive rotations which we haven't explored potentially foldable into a unified rotation-based cache quantization path. proposed scope for a kv cache pr: 1. class implementing the interface 2. 5-bit codes for k and v with per-head-group scales 3. hadamard rotation on before quantization (shared utility) 4. test suite: ppl correctness memory reduction latency benchmarks 5. docs example in 6. benchmarks on llama-3.1-8b and qwen3-7b comparing with if that lands cleanly and shows clear wins vs quanto, weight quantization can follow later based on ecosystem pull. does this scope work for you? happy to start the pr once we have your ack on direction. re phi-3 padding qwen layer quirks both points are extremely useful, thank you. phi-3-mini headdim 96: yes we handle this by zero-padding to 128 before the transform and slicing back after. confirmed no quality penalty in our testing either. i'll document this clearly in the implementation so non-power-of-2 head dims just work . qwen first last 2 layers uncompressed: this aligns with what another tester ( arien0 ( independently reported today on qwen3.5 weight quantization. their broader list of precision-sensitive layers: - - (mtp head) - - - - first last 2 decoder layers (your observation) would you mind if i tag you on the kv cache pr when it's up? you've clearly tested more edge cases than i have a cross-check against the e8 lattice vq approach would be valuable. also curious: how did e8 lattice vq compare to scalar lloyd-max at equivalent bits on qwen2.5-7b? happy to compare notes if you're open to it."]

followed the same pattern suggested fix add a backwards-compatibility shim in : since the function was removed because pytorch 2.1 is now the minimum (where is always available), returning is correct. this would unbreak all models that reference this symbol without any behavioral change. alternatively, a could be emitted to nudge model authors to update their code. expected behavior loading models "excellent initiative, thank you! i am both a user and a developer, but primarily a downstream developer where i maintain small-text ( which uses setfit, which in turn uses sentence-transformers, which uses transformers. so this is an integration use case, where i often juggle around different combinations of these libraries, thereby encountering a lot of different errors. for users - optional functionality should not cause errors: optional features should never throw errors if unused. dependency upgrades are always an adventure, and i regularly get annoyed whenever something fails just because i have, for example, not the right huggingface-hub version (despite not using any hub functionality). a well-placed check or slightly adjusted import location could completely prevent such errors. since huggingface-hub might not be seen as optional from hf's point of view, another example would be wandb. not every user will be interested in every integration. for developers - complex methods and gpu memory: in my active learning use scenarios, i have a scenario of repeated training within the same process, i.e. long-running programs with multiple trainer calls. occasionally (and seemingly random), i suffer from gpu out-of-memory situations, which don't make sense at first glance: gpu-memory-wise, it should not be a problem because the same batch size had been working for many hours (in the same as well in previous processes) before, and moreover, those errors occur only sometimes. upon closer investigation i found that this is often just unlucky timing of the garbage collection, which prevents gpu memory from being freed, often owed to overly long and complex methods in the trainer. smaller methods would decrease the local variable context and make it possible to free references to gpu tensors. just a few days ago, as so often i had to override parts of the trainer adding calls as well as explicit -statement to reduce my out-of-memory problems. this is a common hack i have been using over the years, which should not be needed. from a developer's perspective, breaking things into smaller, clear methods is rarely a downside. my use case might be niche, but long running applications will probably not be that uncommon. edit: slightly clarified my wording to make it more readable.", "provide hfusemlx 0 public flag to disable mlx backend detection at import time summary transformers' import-utils module probes for mlx availability during its own load and imports for backend type checks if is installed. there is no documented opt-out. on some apple silicon configurations, aborts the interpreter (silent during native init), taking the whole host process down before user code can react. context the current backend-detection code path (paraphrased from ): and exist as opt-outs for those backends, but no equivalent for mlx. setting (or any equivalent public knob) would let downstream projects that explicitly do not use mlx prevent the import probe entirely. why a public flag matters here some downstream projects target apple silicon but use pytorch (eager-mode mps) exclusively, with installed only as a transitive dependency or development-time artefact. on those hosts, the import probe is pure cost and can be unsafe; today the only options are: 1. uninstall not always feasible (transitive deps). 2. patch transformers internals what we currently do, against our own no-library-patches rule. brittle on every transformers upgrade. 3. wrap every transformers import in a sandboxed subprocess extreme. a documented (or ) collapses all three into one line. proposed change mirror the existing shape: with documentation in the env-var reference page. workaround in use today a installed before any transformers import that: 1. blocks imports (raises ). 2. wraps the transformers loader and writes after exec. source: docling-project link to import_safety.py and adr 0003 fill in after publishing this issue . acceptance criteria for closing this on our side 1. transformers ships an env var or public flag to disable mlx detection. 2. the flag is documented. 3. we can drop our and use the flag."]
